rulings.Case Title: ROSS DRESS FOR LESS, INC., A VIRGINIA CORPORATION VS. MODESTO RETAIL GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 25, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFF ROSS DRESS FOR LESS, INC., MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Motion granted as to 1st cause of action for declaratory relief. Spreadsheet, Exh. C to Taheri declaration, not admissible. EC section 119. The plain meaning of 6.1.3(d) read in context of the entire section supports the contention that the lease provided no time limitation on the Secondary Reduced Occupancy Period (SROP). Defendant's extrinsic evidence does not demonstrate that his provision is capable of a different interpretation. The first 120 days of the period between 12/1/05 and 6/15/06 (Ace Hardware) do not qualify as an exempted discontinuance. Bi-Rite did not close for any reason which would qualify as an exempted discontinuance. Ace Hardware did not close due to repairs/remodeling because it did not open until 6/15/06. Ace Hardware did not vacate for a casualty, taking, or repairs/remodeling. 6.1.3(f). Motion denied as to the 3rd cause of action for restitution as a triable issue of fact exists. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF NEW YORK AS TRUSTEE FOR THE VS. ANTHONY SMITH-WINTERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Order Granting Attorney'S Fees; Request For Judicail Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 25, 2010, line 9, PLAINTIFF BANK OF NEW YORK Motion to Vacate Order Granting Attorney's Fees. Motion granted. Defendant's second motion for attorney fees was an improper motion for reconsideration. The court initially denied defendant's request for attorney fees based on San Francisco Administrative Code section 37.10B(c)(6) on the grounds that the provision is unconstitutional and violative of the Equal Protection Clause. Defendant's second motion for attorney fees asserted the same basis for fee recovery, was untimely, and brought before a different judge. The order is set aside. CCP section 473(d). =(302/CWW)
